differences.title: 1. DATA MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Predefined schemas enforce data integrity and relationships.
differences.rightDesc: Dynamic schemas allow for varied data structures.

differences.title: 2. SCHEMA
differences.leftDesc: Schema must be defined before data insertion.
differences.rightDesc: Schema can change per document, easy iteration.

differences.title: 3. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Scales well vertically; horizontal scaling complex.
differences.rightDesc: Designed for horizontal scaling across distributed systems.

differences.title: 4. QUERYING
differences.leftDesc: SQL offers complex joins, aggregations, and ACID transactions.
differences.rightDesc: Querying based on document structure, often simpler.

differences.title: 5. DATA INTEGRITY
differences.leftDesc: Strongly ensures atomicity, consistency, isolation, durability.

differences.title: 6. RELATIONSHIPS
differences.leftDesc: Defines relationships via foreign keys and joins.
differences.rightDesc: Data can be nested within documents.

differences.title: 7. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for financial, ERP, and systems requiring strict structure.
differences.rightDesc: Great for content management, catalogs, and fast prototyping.
